# Contributing

- Canonical docs path: `docs/contributing/index.md`
- Route group: `help`
- Section: `Contributing`
- Category: `contributing`
- Page kind: `resource`
- Status: `planned`
- Locale policy: `mirror-to-zh-TW`

## Purpose

Help readers understand when and how to contribute to the PikaCSS repository.

## Target reader

- Contributors who want to improve code, docs, tests, or repository workflows.

## Prerequisites

- Route stage: The reader has reached a repository task, escalation path, or contribution decision point.
- Capability: The reader has basic familiarity with the project or a concrete issue to work on.

## Must include

- What the main contribution paths are.
- Where repository-wide contributor expectations live.
- How readers should choose between consumer docs, plugin authoring, and repository contribution tasks.
- Why validation and scope discipline matter for contribution work.

## Example requirement

- No example required; concise workflow references are more important.

## Must not include

- Detailed implementation tutorials already covered by Learn or Extend.
- Internal-only policy from repository control files.

## Link contract

- Incoming route-local: `../troubleshooting/index.md` and `../skills/index.md`.
- Incoming cross-route: Home page support links and escalation paths from Learn, Extend, or Reference pages.
- Outgoing route-local: `../skills/index.md`.
- Outgoing cross-route: Plugin Development pages, relevant Learn pages for the contribution area, and API Reference when contributors need exact exported surfaces.

## Source of truth

- `AGENTS.md`
- root `README.md`
- package-level READMEs and scripts relevant to contribution workflows

## Notes

- Keep this page focused on public contribution routing.
